trends.name: AI Sweeping Through Information Space
trends.description: Generative AI disrupting the information ecosystem simultaneously across content production, search, distribution, and trust — with implications for media sustainability, information reliability, and democratic discourse.

trends.name: Platform Referral Model Ending for Publishers
trends.description: Social media platform algorithm changes reducing outbound link sharing, cutting referral traffic to news publishers — ending the model where social platforms were a primary audience acquisition channel.

signals.name: Majority of Internet Content Synthetically Produced by 2026
signals.description: Forecasts suggesting the vast majority of all internet content will be AI-synthetically produced by 2026 — with profound implications for information trust, content quality, and human creative employment.

trends.name: Goldilocks Era Ending for Financial Markets
trends.description: The Goldilocks economic environment of 2023 (strong growth + falling inflation) not sustainable into 2024 — with higher-for-longer rates, slowing growth, and ESG regulatory expansion creating a more complex investment landscape.

signals.name: ESG Bond Market Growth Moderating
signals.description: Green and sustainable bond market growth slowing from peak issuance levels — with regulatory scrutiny, greenwashing concerns, and higher rates creating a more selective investor environment for ESG fixed income.

signals.name: 2024 AGM Season Testing Shareholder Climate Engagement
signals.description: 2024 annual general meeting season expected to test the durability of shareholder climate activism — with rising ESG fatigue and political pressure potentially reducing coordinated investor action on climate issues.

trends.name: AI Widely Implemented Across Enterprise
trends.description: Enterprise AI moving from experimentation to wide implementation — with the majority of organizations reporting AI broadly deployed and driving operational outcomes rather than isolated in pilot programs.

trends.name: Generative AI Eclipsing All Other AI Categories
trends.description: Generative AI rapidly dominating organizational AI investment and attention — eclipsing traditional ML, RPA, and predictive analytics as the primary focus of enterprise AI strategy and spending.

signals.name: GPU Availability as AI Scaling Bottleneck
signals.description: GPU scarcity emerging as a material constraint on AI project scaling — with organizations unable to access sufficient compute to match their AI ambitions, driving alternative optimization strategies.

signals.name: AI Environmental Practices Shaping Enterprise AI Strategy
signals.description: Environmental impact of AI infrastructure becoming a factor in enterprise AI decision-making — with sustainability considerations influencing cloud provider selection, model efficiency requirements, and AI governance frameworks.

entries.filename: S&P Global - APAC Sector Roundup Q1 2024.pdf

trends.name: Slowing Dragons, Roaring Tigers in APAC
trends.description: APAC economic growth prospects shifting from China/Japan/Australia toward Southeast Asia and India — with Taiwan, Vietnam, Singapore, and India accelerating as China slows, reshaping the region's investment geography.

signals.name: China Property Crisis Threatening APAC Growth
signals.description: China's property sector distress creating downside scenario risk for regional APAC growth — with credit tightening, demand weakness, and developer defaults having potential systemic spillover effects.

scenarios.name: China Property Crisis Deepening Scenario
scenarios.description: S&P's downside scenario where China's property crisis deepens significantly — with cascading effects on domestic consumption, banking system stability, and regional trade that could substantially slow Asian economic growth.

trends.name: New Risks, New Playbook for Credit Markets
trends.description: Return to higher real interest rates ending the cheap money era — requiring fundamentally different credit analysis, liquidity management, and issuer strategy as refinancing costs rise and economic activity slows.

signals.name: Maturity Wall Approaching for Leveraged Borrowers
signals.description: Significant volume of debt maturities approaching in 2024-2026 — creating refinancing stress for leveraged borrowers who locked in cheap money during 2020-2021 and must now refinance at materially higher rates.

trends.name: Four Global Structural Shifts Defining the Decade
trends.description: Santander identifying four simultaneous global structural shifts — in energy, geopolitics, technology use, and financing access — requiring investors to extend horizons beyond short-term rate cycles.

signals.name: Post-Pandemic Economy Defying Historical Patterns
signals.description: The fastest rate increase in decades producing slower-than-historical growth moderation — challenging conventional macro models and creating genuine uncertainty about the interest rate transmission mechanism.

trends.name: Trump 47 Repricing Market Expectations
trends.description: Donald Trump's 2024 election victory causing markets to reprice US growth expectations higher — with investors weighing whether pro-growth policies can offset inflationary forces of his protectionist agenda.

signals.name: US Fed Soft Landing Achieved
signals.description: US Federal Reserve delivering a soft landing — robust growth alongside falling inflation allowing rate cuts — marking one of the most successful central bank policy navigations in modern history.

trends.name: Industrial Metaverse Evolving from IIoT to Digital Twin
trends.description: Industrial technology evolving through successive layers — from Industrial IoT to digital twin to industrial metaverse — with each layer adding richer simulation, collaboration, and predictive capability for manufacturing operations.

signals.name: Industrial Metaverse Market Rapidly Segmenting by Maturity
signals.description: Industrial metaverse market segmenting between early adopters deploying full digital twin and simulation capabilities and mainstream organizations still building foundational IIoT infrastructure — creating two distinct competitive environments.

entries.filename: SMALLWORLD - How Brands Win In The Entertainment Era_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: Entertainment as Brand Strategy
trends.description: Brands winning by becoming genuine entertainment entities — earning share of voice through creativity and cultural relevance rather than buying it through paid media, competing on entertainment value against legacy brand effects.

signals.name: Eight Entertainment Brand Archetypes Emerging
signals.description: Smallworld identifying eight distinct brand entertainment archetypes — demonstrating that entertainment strategy is not monolithic but requires brands to find their authentic entertainer persona for cultural resonance.
